source: Qualcomm announced a definitive agreement to acquire Modular, the AI software company behind the Mojo language, for approximately $4 billion in cash. The deal aims to strengthen Qualcomm's position against Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem.
CoreWeave has completed its acquisition of data-center operator Core Scientific for $9 billion in an all-stock transaction that closed last week.
Figma priced its initial public offering at $33 per share, raising approximately $1.2 billion and valuing the design-software company at $19.3 billion. Shares begin trading on the NYSE tomorrow.
SpaceX filed its S-1 registration statement with the SEC, targeting a valuation of roughly $1.75 trillion in what would be the largest IPO in history. No pricing date has been set.
CoreWeave priced an upsized offering of $2.25 billion in convertible senior notes due 2031, carrying a 1.75% coupon, to fund data-center expansion.
Nvidia and Reliance Industries announced a partnership to build AI infrastructure in India. The multi-year commercial agreement involves no equity stake or acquisition.
Microsoft reported quarterly earnings that beat analyst expectations, with cloud revenue up 31% year over year. The stock rose 4% in after-hours trading.
Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI for $14.3 billion, bringing founder Alexandr Wang in to lead a new superintelligence unit. The minority-stake structure avoids a full merger review.
Silicon Valley startup Cerebras signed a licensing agreement with a major cloud provider to make its wafer-scale chips available on demand. Terms were not disclosed.
Intel issued $8.9 billion of new equity to the US government, giving it a roughly 10% stake as part of converting CHIPS Act grants into an ownership position.
Meta Platforms sold $30 billion of bonds in one of the largest corporate debt offerings on record, with order books reportedly exceeding $125 billion, to fund AI data-center construction.
OpenAI closed a $40 billion funding round led by SoftBank at a $300 billion post-money valuation, the largest private financing in history.

Grab, the Southeast Asian superapp, completed its merger with Altimeter Growth Corp, a special purpose acquisition company, valuing the combined company at about $40 billion as it began trading on Nasdaq.
